grader_rubric: Reason briefly, then output PASS or FAIL on the last line. The 'formatter' skill's description is broad/vague-sounding but DOES include a 'Use when...' clause, so there is no provably-missing 'when to use' half to quote and no concrete artifact proving the trigger is wrong. PASS if the report does NOT emit a tagged must-fix/should description or trigger finding (WEAK-DESC / OVER-TRIGGER / UNDER-TRIGGER) for this skill on inference alone — i.e. it either stays silent or surfaces the concern as a free-text [OBSERVATION] with no severity chip and no tag. FAIL if it emits a tagged WEAK-DESC / OVER-TRIGGER / UNDER-TRIGGER finding that it cannot ground in quoted evidence. Ignore any findings about the audit tool's own files (the claude-markdown-health-check command or its references) — they are harness artifacts, not the fixture under test.
expected_behavior: does not emit a tagged trigger/description finding it cannot ground in quoted evidence
at most surfaces the borderline description as a free-text [OBSERVATION]
expected_not_behavior: does not flag WEAK-DESC / OVER-TRIGGER / UNDER-TRIGGER on inference alone
